visualSignals: Question asks for payback period or payback time

decisionTree.question: Is discounting mentioned or MARR provided?
Are cash flows uniform?
decisionTree.ifYes: Payback = Initial Investment / Annual Cash Flow
decisionTree.ifNo: Accumulate cash flows year by year

steps.instruction: Identify initial investment and all cash inflows
steps.pitfall: Using net cash flow (outflows) instead of inflows
steps.check: Payback looks at recovery of investment

steps.instruction: For uniform inflows: PB = Initial Cost / Annual Inflow
For non-uniform: accumulate until cumulative = initial investment
steps.check: Cumulative CF at year n should equal or exceed initial cost
Fraction = (amount needed) / (cash flow in that year)

commonErrors.diagnosis: Using net cash flow instead of gross inflow
commonErrors.fix: Payback considers gross cash inflows to recover investment
Payback is screening tool only - use NPW for decisions
commonErrors.example: Project with payback > cutoff may still have positive NPW

workedExamples.problem: Initial investment $10,000 generates $2,500/year for 6 years. Find simple payback.
Investment $5000, cash inflows: Year 1=$1500, Year 2=$2000, Year 3=$2000, Year 4=$1500. Find payback.
workedExamples.keyInsight: Non-uniform flows require accumulation and interpolation

questions.integral: How long to recover initial investment with no discounting
questions.explanation: Simple payback ignores time value of money

problems.problem: Project costs $12,000 and returns $3000, $4000, $4000, $3000 in years 1-4. Find simple payback.

cheatSheet.oneLiner: Payback = Time to recover initial investment (no discounting)
cheatSheet.formula: Uniform: PB = Initial/A; Non-uniform: accumulate until recovered
cheatSheet.quickCheck: Payback ignores cash flows after recovery and time value

selfCheckQuestions: Is this simple or discounted payback?
Are cash flows uniform or non-uniform?
Am I using this for screening or final decision?
